summary: The current app sets several AutoPlay event handlers to MSTakeNoAction under Explorer AutoplayHandlers. Microsoft Learn now documents the exact AutoplayHandlers registry values and the MSTakeNoAction mapping, so this record can be validated.

setting.name: AutoPlay Event Default Action
setting.casual_explanation: Windows can remember what to do when a device or media arrives. This tweak tries to change those remembered choices so common AutoPlay events take no action.
setting.why_people_change_it: People change it when they want removable media, cameras, or DVDs to stop auto-opening apps or action prompts.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Setting common handlers to take no action can make device arrival calmer, but it is currently documented here as a user-preference write, not as an officially mapped policy surface.
setting.professional_notes: The local AutoPlay.adml help text for the DontSetAutoplayCheckbox policy confirms that AutoPlay can remember a user's choice of what to do when a device is connected. Microsoft Learn's Windows settings reference documents HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\AutoplayHandlers and lists MSTAKENOACTION for the relevant event-handler paths. The current app writes the same value into multiple UserChosenExecuteHandlers and EventHandlersDefaultSelection paths.

allowed_values.label: No explicit handler choice stored
allowed_values.meaning: Windows falls back to its normal AutoPlay behavior and remembered-choice state.

allowed_values.label: Take no action
allowed_values.meaning: Observed app write intended to make the selected AutoPlay event take no action.

targets.notes: This record treats MSTakeNoAction as an observed user-choice value, not an officially documented policy mapping.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes MSTakeNoAction into multiple AutoplayHandlers paths for common media and device-arrival events.

windows_defaults.label: Windows AutoPlay default
windows_defaults.applies_to: Windows user profiles that have not stored explicit per-event handler choices

states.rationale: Do not publish the observed per-event handler value as an official baseline.

windows_defaults.notes: Microsoft documents that AutoPlay can remember user choices and now documents the exact handler registry mapping as official.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
avoid_for: People who want a reviewed no-action handler mapping today

states.rationale: Keep the record honest until the per-event handler mapping is officially sourced.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want to avoid publishing an unsourced AutoPlay handler mapping as validated.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want a reviewed and sourced 'take no action' behavior for specific device events.
tradeoffs: Windows keeps its normal AutoPlay choice behavior.
The current app write remains documented only as an observed preference.

recommended_profiles.label: Current app profile

states.rationale: Observed app value for common AutoPlay events.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You are documenting what the app currently writes.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You need a different AutoPlay behavior for specific device events.
tradeoffs: The desired no-action intent is clear.
The exact handler registry mapping is now officially documented.

evidence.title: Microsoft Learn: Windows settings reference - AutoplayHandlers registry values
evidence.summary: Microsoft Learn documents HKCU\Software\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\Explorer\AutoplayHandlers and lists MSTAKENOACTION for the relevant event-handler paths.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft AutoPlay.adml remembered-choice guidance
evidence.summary: The local ADML help text for Prevent AutoPlay from remembering user choices explains that AutoPlay can remember what a user chooses to do when a device is connected.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes MSTakeNoAction to multiple Explorer AutoplayHandlers per-event paths such as StorageOnArrival and camera or DVD events.

decision.why: Microsoft Learn documents the AutoplayHandlers registry values and the MSTAKENOACTION mapping for the relevant per-event paths.

validation_proof.notes: Validated against Microsoft Learn's Windows settings reference for AutoplayHandlers registry values.
